title: QA Test Report
description: Structured output from QA Engineer using write-tests, frontend-testing, or review-service skills. Emitted after test execution. Consumed by Technical Lead, Reviewer, Project Manager, and Agent Coordinator as release confidence evidence. Complements code-review-finding.json — review catches code issues, test-report catches behavior risk.

delivery_plan_ref.description: technical-delivery-plan.json plan_id this test run validates.

environment.description: Test environment name (e.g. staging, preview, local, production-canary).

test_types_executed.description: Types of tests run in this report.

scenarios_executed.description: One entry per AC scenario or test case executed.

name.description: Scenario name — should match feature-ticket AC scenario label when covering AC.

evidence.description: CI run link, screenshot path, log excerpt, or 'manual verified' description.

defects.description: Defects found during testing. Empty array if none.

coverage_gaps.description: Behaviors or edge cases that could not be tested in this cycle and why.

residual_risk.description: Remaining risk that won't be covered before release — should be visible to Technical Lead and PM.

release_recommendation.description: QA recommendation on whether to proceed with release.

scenarios_executed.name: COD order assigned to store courier
COD order with no store courier falls back
scenarios_executed.evidence: CI run #1042, test NewLogicAutoAssignTest::testCodOrderFallsBackToWarehouse — passed; warning log verified

scenarios_executed.name: Non-COD order unaffected
scenarios_executed.evidence: CI run #1042 — all existing non-COD tests pass without modification

scenarios_executed.name: Store service 503 fallback (code-review F-01)
scenarios_executed.evidence: Manual test with store-service stub returning 503 — fallback to warehouse confirmed; error logged with order_id
scenarios_executed.notes: Added after code-review finding F-01 raised by Reviewer

coverage_gaps: Load test for COD routing under high batch volume — deferred to next sprint
examples.residual_risk: Batch performance under > 500 concurrent COD orders not validated. Low risk given current store volume.
